perturbation.original_text: This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties and supersedes all prior communications, agreements and understandings relating to the subject matter hereof. The provisions of this Agreement may not be modified, amended nor waived, except by a written instrument duly executed by both parties. This Agreement may not be assigned by Receiving Party without the prior written consent of the MyPoints.com.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party agrees that receipt of Confidential Information, pursuant to this Agreement, is exclusively for the purpose of evaluating a potential acquisition of <PAGE> MyPoints.com and Receiving Party shall not use the Confidential Information for any other purpose. This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement of the parties and supersedes all prior communications, agreements and understandings relating to the subject matter hereof. The provisions of this Agreement may not be modified, amended nor waived, except by a written instrument duly executed by both parties. This Agreement may not be assigned by Receiving Party without the prior written consent of the MyPoints.com.
perturbation.explanation: The integration clause is moved to paragraph 2, regarding purpose, making it less visible.
perturbation.contradicted_law: § 2-202. Final Written Expression: Parol or Extrinsic Evidence
perturbation.law_explanation: By placing the integration clause in an unrelated section, its enforceability is reduced. UCC 2-202 emphasizes that terms in a confirmatory memorandum as final are not able to be contradicted by evidence of any prior agreement. By moving the integration clause to an irrelevant section of the contract, a party could argue that the clause was not properly presented, or should be subordinate to the overall intentions of the contract.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2-202. Final Written Expression: Parol or Extrinsic Evidence. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 2-202. Final Written Expression: Parol or Extrinsic Evidence. Terms with respect to which the confirmatory memoranda of the parties agree or which are otherwise set forth in a writing intended by the parties as a final expression of their agreement with respect to such terms as are included therein may not be contradicted by evidence of any prior agreement or of a contemporaneous oral agreement but may be explained or supplemented (a) by course of dealing or usage of trade (Section 1-205 ) or by course of performance (Section 2-208 ); and (b) by evidence of consistent additional terms unless the court finds the writing to have been intended also as a complete and exclusive statement of the terms of the agreement . Uniform Commercial Code Toolbox About Uniform Laws State Uniform Commercial Codes
perturbation.justification: The perturbation is categorized as a 'Structural Flaws Legal Contradiction' where a legally required disclosure is placed in an irrelevant or misleading section. The original contract has an integration clause which is critical for ensuring that the written agreement represents the complete and final understanding between the parties. By moving this clause to the purpose section, it becomes less visible and could be argued as not being properly presented, potentially undermining its enforceability. The Law Explanation suggests that UCC 2-202 emphasizes the finality of written agreements, and moving the integration clause weakens this. Scraped Law Snippet 1 supports this by stating that terms intended as a final expression of the agreement cannot be contradicted by prior agreements. By relocating the integration clause, a party might argue it's subordinate to the overall intention or not a central term, thus contradicting the law's intent to ensure finality and enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party agrees to keep the existence and nature of this Agreement confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: Any material provided by MyPoints.com to Receiving Party which is clearly designated "Confidential" (or other similar legend) will be presumed to be Confidential Information. Receiving Party agrees to keep the existence and nature of this Agreement confidential.
perturbation.explanation: The confidentiality clause is moved to paragraph 1, regarding the definition of Confidential Information.
perturbation.law_explanation: By including the promise to maintain the confidentiality of the agreement with the definition of Confidential Information, there may be ambiguity that the agreement's existence is only considered confidential if labeled as such. This contrasts to the broad obligation for good faith in the UCC, where the party benefiting from this error could take advantage of it in the process.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 1-201. General Definitions. | Uniform Commercial Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you § 1-201. General Definitions. (a) Unless the context otherwise requires, words or phrases defined in this section, or in the additional definitions contained in other articles of the Uniform Commercial Code that apply to particular articles or parts thereof, have the meanings stated. (b) Subject to definitions contained in other articles of the Uniform Commercial Code that apply to particular articles or parts thereof: (1) " Action ", in the sense of a judicial proceeding, includes recoupment, counterclaim, set-off, suit in equity, and any other proceeding in which rights are determined. (2) " Aggrieved party " means a party entitled to pursue a remedy . (3) " Agreement ", as distinguished from " contract ", means the bargain of the parties in fact, as found in their language or inferred from other circumstances, including course of performance, course of dealing, or usage of trade as provided in Section 1-303. (4) " Bank " means a person engaged in the business of banking and includes a savings bank, savings and loan association, credit union, and trust company. (5) " Bearer " means a person in possession of a negotiable instrument, document of title , or certificated security that is payable to bearer or indorsed in blank. (6) " Bill of lading " means a document evidencing the receipt of goods for shipment issued by a person engaged in the business of transporting or forwarding goods. (7) " Branch " includes a separately incorporated foreign branch of a bank . (8) " Burden of establishing " a fact means the burden of persuading the trier of fact that the existence of the fact is more probable than its nonexistence. (9) " Buyer in ordinary course of business " means a person that buys goods in good faith , without knowledge that the sale violates the rights of another person in the goods, and in the ordinary course from a per
perturbation.justification: The modified text could be interpreted to mean that the agreement's confidentiality is only protected if explicitly labeled as 'Confidential,' which contradicts the original text's broader confidentiality obligation. The LLM explanation notes that this ambiguity could be exploited, especially in light of the UCC's implied duty of good faith. Snippet 1 defines 'agreement' broadly under the UCC, implying that the parties' intent and circumstances surrounding the agreement are relevant, which could be undermined by a narrow interpretation of confidentiality based solely on labeling. This creates a structural flaw that can affect its enforceability.
